data.body:
G4 container child-consistency audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child fe57 "Hermetic-but-honest Jira fixtures + verified fake client"), cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937). All 7 cross-child interaction modes checked under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter. VERDICT: clean pairing — no G4 finding.

fe57 is the FOUNDATION story; its file_impact (tests/fixtures/jira/ [new], tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/_fakes.py [new FakeAcliClient], scripts/capture_jira_fixtures.py [new]) is DISJOINT from every sibling's edits: 8937 (_snapshot_schema.py/test_snapshot_contract.py/fetcher.py), 59ef (test_acliclient_contract.py/acli_graph.py), 33d0 (apply_outbound.py/batch_dispatch.py/applier.py), 2359 (batch_dispatch.py/applier.py/reconcile.py), 822a (e2e_field_validation_probe.sh/test_roundtrip.py). 8937, 59ef, and 822a each declare depends_on fe57.

7-mode walk:
- Implicit shared state — BENIGN. fe57's three files are net-new and disjoint from all siblings. The FakeAcliClient is PRODUCED by fe57 and CONSUMED (read) by 59ef/8937/822a — a clean producer/consumer split, not a shared mutable write.
- Conflicting assumptions — BENIGN. fe57 AC2 ("fake serves fixtures through production fetch_snapshot enrichment with NO shape transformation") is mutually reinforcing with 8937 (snapshot-entry schema targets exactly those unmassaged shapes), 59ef AC2 (an unmassaged fixture-faithful fake is precisely what passes the return-shape contract), and 822a (round-trips those shapes). fe57 covers the INBOUND fetch/enrichment surface; the 33d0↔2359 outbound labels list-vs-count representation mismatch is outbound-telemetry-internal and untouched by fe57.
- Dependency gap — BENIGN. fe57 is the root foundation needing no sibling input, so correctly declares no depends_on; the three downstream consumers correctly declare depends_on fe57. No missing input edge.
- Scope overlap — BENIGN. fe57 owns fixtures + fake + capture/scrub script + re-capture doc and explicitly scopes OUT the contract test (→8937) and the verified-fake honesty check (→59ef), consistent with the roster. Distinct surface from every sibling.
- Ordering violation — BENIGN. The only hard ordering edge (fixtures/fake must exist before the tests that consume them) is captured by the three declared depends_on fe57 edges. fe57 is not entangled in the 33d0↔2359 outbound-applier same-region sequencing cluster (different files/surface).
- Consumer impact — BENIGN. The fake adds NO shape massaging (AC2/AC4), so consumers receive exactly the production-fetcher shapes — this ENABLES rather than constrains siblings. The re-capture doc (AC3) guards fixtures against change-detector rot, consistent with parent AC7.
- Residual references — BENIGN. fe57 references only the inbound fetch/enrichment surface. 33d0's deletion of the dead typed-leaf OUTBOUND applier strands nothing fe57 references.

The roster-level parent-AC6 absence (documented anti-change-detector guardrail GOVERNING new tests) is a G3 coverage matter already tracked across the sibling set and on this ticket's prior G3 audits — it is not a G4 cross-child inconsistency, so it is not re-filed here.

VERDICT for this pairing (parent × fe57): no G4 cross-child finding; all 7 interaction modes read benign under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter.
